description:
Offered: every other year
Not all literature is the plot-based, character-driven storytelling we know and love. This course explores techniques and genres of writing that are open to creative experimentation, play, and risk-taking. Students read literature that breaks from convention and write creative pieces that push the boundaries of storytelling. These could include cross-genre and multimedia work, anti-narrative writing, appropriated/found writing, generative literature, and constraint writing. Students’ creative works are deliberate experiments that hypothesize and reflect upon the effect of their choices as writers.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200 or HON 1200
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, PL
